comments.body: In my spring-form.tld, I've this line :<uri>springframework.org/tags/form</uri>

answers.body:
If you open up spring-webmvc-3.1.0-RELEASE.jar/META-INF/spring-form.tld the <uri> to use is 

<uri>http://www.springframework.org/tags/form</uri>

So you should have

<%@ taglib prefix="form" uri="http://www.springframework.org/tags/form" %>

comments.body: Don't think this is the right answer, spring form is in spring-webmvc which according to OP is already a dependency.
They already have that library in their classpath. Also, that library is not required for the taglib they're using.
<version>Your Security Version</version> what i should give in this? its not working for me, I am trying so long please anyone suggest

answers.body:
Add this dependency in pom.xml

<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-security-taglibs</artifactId>
    <version>Your Security Version</version>
</dependency>

I run into similar issue, but for packed war with embedded Tomcat.

Solution was to whitelist the spring-webmvc library in TLD Scanning:

@Configuration
public class EmbeddedTomcatConfig {

    @Bean
    public TomcatServletWebServerFactory tomcatFactory() {
        return new TomcatServletWebServerFactory() {

            @Override
            protected void postProcessContext(Context context) {
                var scanner = context.getJarScanner();

                if (scanner instanceof StandardJarScanner standardScanner) {
                    StandardJarScanFilter filter = new StandardJarScanFilter();

                    filter.setTldSkip("*");
                    filter.setTldScan("spring-webmvc-*.jar);

                    standardScanner.setJarScanFilter(filter);
                }
            }
        };
    }
}

title: The absolute uri: http://www.springsource.org/tags/form cannot be resolved in either web.xml or the jar files deployed with this application
body:
Currently, I use a 'Maven' project where I put in my pom.xml the current dependencies to use Spring Framework where org.springframework.version = 3.1.0.RELEASE : 

<dependency>
   <groupId>org.springframework</groupId>
   <artifactId>spring-core</artifactId>
   <version>${org.springframework.version}</version>
</dependency>

<dependency>
   <groupId>org.springframework</groupId>
   <artifactId>spring-context</artifactId>
   <version>${org.springframework.version}</version>
</dependency>

<!-- Spring MVC framework -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-webmvc</artifactId>
    <version>${org.springframework.version}</version>
</dependency>

<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-security-taglibs</artifactId>
    <version>${org.springframework.version}</version>
</dependency>

All the jar dependencies are sitting in target/myGoogleAppEngine-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT/WEB-INF/lib. This directory contains :

spring-aop-3.1.0.RELEASE.jar
spring-beans-3.1.0.RELEASE.jar
spring-webmvc-3.1.0.RELEASE.jar
spring-web-3.1.0.RELEASE.jar
spring-security-taglibs-3.1.0.RELEASE.jar
spring-security-web-3.1.0.RELEASE.jar
...

At the beginning of my address.jsp, I've the following line : 

<%@ taglib prefix="form" uri="http://www.springsource.org/tags/form" %>

But I've the following error when I execute the address.jsp : 

Error 500
org.apache.jasper.JasperException: The absolute uri: http://www.springsource.org/tags/form cannot be resolved in either web.xml or the jar files deployed with this application
